page: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dlsv7 server family. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and is equipped with a substantial 480 MB L3 cache. It includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. The system is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, relying instead on remote Premium Storage options.

In benchmark evaluations, the D64ls_v7 demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities and high memory bandwidth. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests ranks in the top tier and strong categories, respectively. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, achieve top-tier performance. Memory bandwidth for both cached and uncached read operations is strong, though the overall PassMark memory score remains average. For specialized workloads, the hardware-accelerated instruction sets deliver strong performance in cryptographic operations and LLM prompt processing, although LLM text generation speeds remain average.

The primary tradeoff of the D64ls_v7 is its resource allocation profile. With only 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU and no local storage, it is not designed for memory-intensive databases or applications requiring high-speed local scratch space. However, this configuration offers high qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads, as users do not pay for idle memory overhead. The instance is highly suited for compute-heavy tasks, including batch processing, web serving, cryptography, multi-threaded decompression, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs (32 physical cores) and 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512. This instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on remote Premium Storage. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-threaded compression and strong multi-core CPU performance, alongside high memory bandwidth. The D64ls_v7 offers high qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads by minimizing memory overhead. It is well-suited for CPU-based machine learning inference, cryptographic processing, multi-threaded decompression, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and advanced AMX instruction sets, designed for high-throughput compute workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v7 is a compute-optimized VM with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and AMX support, built for high-performance compute tasks.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-optimized virtual machine featuring advanced matrix extensions and a lean memory profile for intensive parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz
Lean memory profile of 128 GB RAM (2 GB per vCPU) optimizing compute-to-cost ratios
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and cryptographic operations
Strong multi-core performance and top-tier single-threaded decompression speeds
No local storage or GPUs, utilizing remote Premium Storage for disk operations
Best suited for CPU-based inference, batch processing, and high-throughput web serving
